title: silene aethiopica subsp. aethiopica
intro: Silene aethiopica subsp. aethiopica, commonly known as the white campion or aethiopic white campion, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and providing relief from menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of Silene aethiopica subsp. aethiopica include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
